stat_of_week: 40 - Sugar Land Skeeters scored 40 runs in a single week, the highest run total of any team this season, powering their dominant 8–1 demolition of Hiroshima Radiation
thriller_note: BaseballPro77 squeaked by with just a one-category margin despite posting a brutal 5.28 ERA and only 2 HR, meaning Benchwarmers — who had better hitting across the board — narrowly missed pulling off the upset, and both teams remain dangerously inconsistent heading into their next tests.
key_storyline: The Wisconsin Rapids Reapers delivered the week's most dominant performance, sweeping the Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs 9–0 behind a historically elite 0.84 WHIP, 75 strikeouts, and Ben Rice's monster line of 9 R, 3 HR, and 9 RBI. The Wonderdogs, meanwhile, posted zero stolen bases and a league-low 0 category wins — a complete organizational collapse that raises serious questions about their roster construction. After being ranked 9th in last week's power rankings with perceived upside, Wisconsin looks like a legitimate top-three contender while Wonder Wharf may be the league's most troubled team.
lucky_reason: Despite a dreadful 4.89 ERA, only 42 strikeouts, and just 46 IP from their pitching staff, the Benchwarmers scraped out a 5–4 win with the league's second-highest luck delta (+0.58), meaning their actual win probability was barely above zero and they benefited enormously from a weak opponent showing up in the right categories.
unlucky_reason: General Hospital posted a sparkling 2.10 ERA, a 1.02 WHIP, a .363 OBP, and 30 runs scored — genuinely elite numbers — yet their luck delta of -0.52 is tied for the worst in the league, indicating their strong stats were almost entirely wasted against a favorable schedule that didn't reward them proportionally.
records_broken: North Correa tied the single-week RBI record with 38 RBI, matching the previous mark set by North Correa themselves in Week 2 — no new record was broken. Abbotsford-Colby Twins tied the single-week strikeout record with 77 K, matching the previous mark of 77 set by Abbotsford-Colby Twins in Week 2 — no new record was broken.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Back-to-back elite weeks, 40 R this week is a season record, Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson both starred — this roster is the class of the league | up
2. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | 9–0 sweep with a 0.84 WHIP and 75 Ks is historically dominant; Ben Rice is a must-watch breakout — this team is ascending fast | up
3. Riverside Roughnecks | 7–3 win with 38 R, 10 HR, and a 2.42 ERA — this is the most complete performance of their young season and they look nothing like last week's pretenders | up
4. North Correa | Oneil Cruz went nuclear with 4 HR and 10 RBI, Ohtani chipped in — 7–3 result with elite power and solid pitching makes this a legitimate contender | up
5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP is absurd and 77 Ks anchors elite pitching; the 6–4 win was workmanlike but this roster has genuine top-tier upside | same
6. General Hospital | A 2.10 ERA and .363 OBP losing 6–4 is a crime; the talent is real and the luck will turn — don't sleep on this team despite the ugly result | down
7. BaseballPro77 | Escaped with a 5–4 squeaker on the back of terrible ERA and only 2 HR — this team is living dangerously, but two weeks of wins is two weeks of wins | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Got destroyed 8–1 in a shocking reversal after looking strong; only 43 Ks and 1 category win is an organizational crisis that needs urgent attention | down
9. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge and James Wood are elite pieces but the pitching (4.30 ERA, 46 IP, 2 QS) is genuinely holding this team back from its ceiling | down
10. Buttville Skidmarks | Lost 7–3 despite decent pitching (3.19 ERA, 4 QS) — the offense simply isn't reliable enough to compete with top teams most weeks | same
11. Canberra Wombats | Lost 6–4 with real offensive contributors in Altuve (.577 OBP), Walker, and Henderson — they're not as bad as their record suggests but pitching keeps killing them | up
12. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison was genuinely brilliant (1.65 ERA, 26 K) but lost 7–3 anyway; heavy waiver activity suggests the manager knows change is needed | same
13. Benchwarmers | A 5–4 win built on smoke and mirrors — 4.89 ERA, 42 Ks, and a historically lucky result; Corbin Carroll is a bright spot on an otherwise fragile roster | same
14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A 9–0 shutout loss with zero stolen bases and a league-worst 0 category wins is as bad as it gets; urgent roster reconstruction is needed | down
